regions.western-caribbean: Western Caribbean conditions this April 1st show steady easterly winds near 10-14 knots with overcast skies and minimal rain at Cozumel. Expect increasing shower activity by late day, rising to scattered thunderstorms with an 85% chance tomorrow and Thursday, especially near Costa Maya and Belize City. Mariners should prepare for rougher seas and localized squalls; small craft operators in Roatan and Grand Cayman should monitor conditions closely and avoid exposed anchorages during thunderstorms. Cruise passengers should plan indoor activities during storms and fishing captains should schedule trips early in the day to avoid afternoon thunderstorm hazards.
regions.bahamas: Bahamas region update for April 1, 2026: Current conditions in Nassau show clear skies, light ENE winds at 5 knots, and a mild 68°F. Expect increasing cloud cover through the day with ENE winds rising to 11 knots and a low 10% chance of rain. By day two, easterly winds strengthen to 21 knots with overcast skies but minimal rainfall; a Small Craft Advisory may be warranted for exposed waters near Freeport and Bimini. Drizzle and lighter temperatures near 75°F are forecast on day three with steady E winds at 21 knots—yacht and fishing captains should secure vessels and monitor for localized seas above 4 feet in open passages. Passengers should plan for cooler mornings and light rain gear for afternoon excursions.
regions.southern-caribbean: Southern Caribbean briefing for April 1, 2026: Aruba reports 79°F with ENE winds at 12 knots and partly cloudy skies; a 28% chance of rain exists mainly from isolated showers. Expect similar conditions through the next three days with light rain showers possible, particularly on Day 1 (35% rain chance) and Day 2; winds will remain steady from the east-northeast at 14-16 knots, increasing slightly but remaining below Small Craft Advisory thresholds. Curacao and Bonaire should experience comparable conditions with moderate trade winds and limited shower activity, while Cartagena remains warm and mostly dry with light easterly breezes. Mariners should secure loose gear and monitor localized squalls; fishing captains can plan nearshore trips but remain vigilant for brief rain showers. Anchorage conditions at Aruba and Curaçao ports remain stable with moderate winds and good visibility, suitable for routine operations.
regions.central-caribbean: Central Caribbean conditions today remain mild with temperatures near 78 to 79 degrees Fahrenheit and easterly winds 13 to 16 knots. Skies are partly cloudy with only a 13 percent chance of rain at Grand Cayman, but thunderstorms will increase in coverage starting tomorrow through day three, with rain chances 41 to 60 percent and persistent east winds 15 to 16 knots. Cruise and fishing captains should prepare for reduced visibility and localized heavy showers, especially near Grand Cayman, Cozumel, and Belize City, where thunderstorm activity will be most frequent. Anchored vessels should ensure secure mooring lines, and mariners are advised to monitor local updates for any Small Craft Advisories as winds approach 20 knots. Passengers should expect intermittent wet conditions and plan outdoor activities accordingly.
regions.lesser-antilles: Lesser Antilles weather on April 1, 2026, features moderate easterly trades at 12-16 knots with partly cloudy to overcast skies and a 15-30 percent chance of scattered showers. Expect temperatures near 78-82°F across Barbados, St. Lucia, Martinique, Dominica, Antigua, St. Kitts, St. Maarten, St. Vincent, and Grenada, with generally stable conditions and no tropical disturbances present. Mariners should note moderate easterly winds favoring east-facing anchorages, but maintain vigilance for brief rain squalls reducing visibility and causing localized gusts. Fishing captains and yacht operators are advised to plan for moderate seas and adjust routes to avoid overcast periods with passing showers, especially near exposed ports like St. Vincent and Dominica.
regions.los-angeles: Los Angeles region ports report mild conditions this morning with 62°F, light WSW winds at 4 knots, and overcast skies. Expect increasing west winds to 12 knots this afternoon with a 30% chance of drizzle, reducing to light overcast and 3% rain on Thursday. Friday will warm noticeably to around 81°F with south-southwest winds near 9 knots and dry skies. Mariners should monitor drizzle and reduced visibility near Catalina Island and maintain caution when approaching Long Beach and Marina del Rey harbors; fishing captains can expect calm seas but plan for light wind shifts over the next two days. Passengers should dress in layers today and Thursday, with lighter clothing suitable for Friday’s warmer temperatures.
regions.ensenada: Ensenada region conditions for April 1, 2026: Current temperatures near 62°F with light easterly winds at 3 knots and overcast skies. Expect west to northwest winds increasing to 9-15 knots over the next two days, remaining overcast with minimal rain chances (below 10%). Mariners should experience smooth seas with no Small Craft Advisories; however, moderate westerly winds may cause slight chop near Punta Banda and Islas Todos Santos. Cruise and fishing captains should plan for stable conditions but maintain awareness of variable WNW winds; anchorage in Ensenada Bay remains sheltered and secure. Passengers advised to dress in layers due to cool morning temps and overcast skies.
regions.cabo-san-lucas: Cabo San Lucas and nearby ports will experience steady NNW to NW winds of 13-17 knots with temperatures in the mid-70s and overcast skies through April 3rd. No precipitation or marine wind warnings are expected, so conditions remain safe for navigation and outdoor activities. Yacht and fishing captains should note moderate winds that could create light chop, particularly near exposed anchorages in La Paz. Passengers are advised to dress in layers due to cooler sea breezes and expect overcast conditions limiting sun exposure.
regions.mazatlan: Mazatlán region will experience mild conditions today with clear skies, light northwest winds near 3 knots, and no precipitation expected. Winds increase to 9-11 knots WNW over the next three days with partly cloudy to overcast skies but rain chances remain negligible. Mariners should expect calm seas and good visibility in Mazatlán, Topolobampo, and Altata harbors, with no marine wind warnings in effect. Cruise and fishing operations can proceed normally; small craft operators should monitor wind shifts from NW to WNW but no advisories are anticipated.
regions.puerto-vallarta: Puerto Vallarta and surrounding ports will see comfortable conditions today with temperatures near 80°F and light northerly winds around 3 knots. Expect mostly overcast skies with westerly winds increasing to 8-9 knots over the next three days, remaining below Small Craft Advisory thresholds. No precipitation is forecast, so fishing and yacht operations in Puerto Vallarta, Punta Mita, Yelapa, and Chacala can proceed without weather-related delays. Mariners should monitor wind shifts when navigating near Punta Mita’s exposed anchorages, but overall conditions remain favorable for cruising and shore excursions.
regions.western-mediterranean:
Western Mediterranean Weather Briefing for April 01, 2026:

Current conditions in Barcelona show mild temperatures at 53°F with light WNW winds around 2 knots and partly cloudy skies. Expect fog development tonight and into tomorrow morning around Barcelona and Valencia, reducing visibility—exercise caution entering or leaving port. By tomorrow, increased southerly winds near 6-8 knots will bring scattered rain showers, especially near Palma de Mallorca and Ibiza, with a 63% chance of rain; small craft operators should monitor local updates but no advisories are currently needed. Conditions improve by April 3 with warmer temperatures near 70°F and light westerly winds, but overcast skies will persist; anchorage conditions remain stable across Malaga, Cadiz, Lisbon, and Gibraltar with calm seas and no significant wind threats. Cruise and yacht captains should plan for reduced visibility early April 2 and prepare for wet decks during showers, while fishing captains should expect calmer seas and moderate winds after the
regions.central-mediterranean: Central Mediterranean outlook for April 1, 2026: Naples reports steady NNE winds at 15-16 knots with overcast skies and a low rain chance early, increasing to a 73% chance of rain later today. Expect persistent moderate northerly winds 8-16 knots across the region through Day 2 with rain showers, easing by Day 3 to mostly overcast and dry conditions. Mariners should secure vessels at exposed anchorages, especially around Naples and Civitavecchia, as winds remain steady but below Small Craft Advisory thresholds. Cruise and yacht operators should prepare for wet decks and reduced visibility; fishing captains are advised to monitor localized rain showers near Sardinia and Corsica before venturing offshore.
regions.eastern-mediterranean:
Eastern Mediterranean Weather Briefing for April 01, 2026:

Expect persistent rain showers across the region today, with Athens/Piraeus reporting 58°F, northeast winds at 14 kt, and 100% rain likelihood. Winds will shift to south-southwest at 11-6 kt by day two and three, with decreasing rain chances but ongoing showers, particularly near Rhodes, Izmir, Cyprus, and Alexandria. Mariners should anticipate wet decks and reduced visibility; small craft should exercise caution in open waters, especially around exposed anchorages like Santorini and Mykonos. Cruise and fishing vessels should secure outdoor equipment and monitor local updates for any sudden wind shifts or intensified showers.
